cmd/jobs/ledger/service.purpose: Pure-Go backfill orchestration service (no Cobra, no direct Ent import in service.go) that pages through customers and provisions missing ledger accounts. Separated from the command layer so it can be unit-tested without a real database.

patterns.name: Interface-driven dependencies, no Ent in service.go
patterns.description: Service depends on unexported customerLister and accountProvisioner interfaces; concrete implementations (EntCustomerLister, real resolver) are injected from the command layer. service.go must never import *entdb.Client.
patterns.example: type customerLister interface { ListCustomers(ctx context.Context, input ListCustomersInput) (ListCustomersResult, error) }

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() and error-returning constructor
patterns.description: All dependencies are bundled in Config, validated in Config.Validate(), and NewService returns (*Service, error). Add new dependencies to Config and validate them — never add optional fields with zero-value fallback.
patterns.example: func NewService(cfg Config) (*Service, error) { if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid backfill config: %w", err) }; return &Service{...}, nil }

patterns.name: RunInput.normalized() for caller-visible defaults
patterns.description: Input defaults (CustomerPageSize 0 -> DefaultCustomerPageSize, CreatedBefore UTC normalization) are applied by a private normalized() method inside Run, not in the command layer or constructor.
patterns.example: func (i RunInput) normalized() RunInput { out := i; if out.CustomerPageSize == 0 { out.CustomerPageSize = DefaultCustomerPageSize }; return out }

patterns.name: paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER guard on all cursor loops
patterns.description: Every cursor-paginated loop bounds iterations by paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER and calls recordFailure + returns when exhausted, preventing infinite loops on bad cursors.
patterns.example: for iter := 0; iter < paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER; iter++ { ... }; if !completed { return result, s.recordFailure(&result, "paginate_customers", "", fmt.Errorf("max safe iter reached")) }

patterns.name: Idempotent Get-then-Create with ValidationIssue code detection
patterns.description: Always call GetCustomerAccounts / GetBusinessAccounts first; only provision when the error carries ledger.ErrCodeCustomerAccountMissing / ErrCodeBusinessAccountMissing via hasValidationIssueCode. Other errors go through recordFailure, making the backfill safely re-runnable.
patterns.example: if !hasValidationIssueCode(err, ledger.ErrCodeCustomerAccountMissing) { return s.recordFailure(result, "get_customer_accounts", customerID.ID, err) }

patterns.name: DryRun increments WouldProvision counters only
patterns.description: When input.DryRun is true, increment BusinessWouldProvision / CustomersWouldProvision and return without calling EnsureBusinessAccounts / CreateCustomerAccounts.

patterns.name: recordFailure owns FailureCount and slog.Warn; ContinueOnError checked by callers
patterns.description: recordFailure always increments result.FailureCount and logs via slog.Warn; callers — not recordFailure — check input.ContinueOnError to decide whether to return the error or continue.
patterns.example: failure := s.recordFailure(&result, "list_customers", "", err); if input.ContinueOnError { return result, nil }; return result, failure

key_file_guides.role: Core orchestration: Config/Service types, customerLister/accountProvisioner interfaces, Run/runNamespace/ensureBusinessAccounts/ensureCustomerAccounts/recordFailure/hasValidationIssueCode.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No direct Ent imports — keep free of *entdb.Client. Do not add new ledger error codes without updating hasValidationIssueCode. ContinueOnError must be checked by callers of recordFailure, not inside it.

key_file_guides.role: EntCustomerLister implements customerLister using Ent cursor queries (query.Cursor) with namespace + DeletedAt + CreatedAt filters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses query.Cursor(ctx, input.Cursor) — cursor, not offset, pagination. IncludeDeleted=false filters via customerdb.Or(DeletedAtIsNil(), DeletedAtGTE(now)); CreatedBefore uses CreatedAtLT with UTC normalization.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests using fakeCustomerLister and fakeAccountProvisioner covering dry-run, cursor pagination, created-before cutoff, and continue-on-error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses t.Context() not context.Background(). fakeCustomerLister implements cursor pagination manually (time+ID ordering); fakeAccountProvisioner emits real ValidationIssue codes via ledger.ErrCustomerAccountMissing.WithAttrs(...).

anti_patterns: Importing *entdb.Client directly in service.go — breaks unit testability
Checking ContinueOnError inside recordFailure — callers own that decision
Skipping the MAX_SAFE_ITER guard in any new paginated loop
Using offset pagination instead of paginationv2.Cursor for customer iteration
Using context.Background() in tests instead of t.Context()

decisions.decision: Separate service package from the Cobra command.
decisions.rationale: Enables pure-Go unit tests (no Ent, no Cobra, no real DB) covering dry-run, cursor paging, cutoff filtering, and error continuation without integration overhead.

decisions.decision: Detect missing-account condition via ValidationIssue error codes, not nil checks.
decisions.rationale: ErrCodeCustomerAccountMissing / ErrCodeBusinessAccountMissing are structured codes on ValidationIssue; treating any non-nil error as 'missing' would mask real DB or network failures.

code_examples.scenario: Cursor-paginated customer provisioning loop with DryRun and ContinueOnError

cmd/jobs/llmcost.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for LLM cost database operations; exposes a single 'sync' command that delegates to the Wire-provided LLMCostSyncJob on internal.App, with no local service construction.

patterns.name: Package-level Cmd var with init() sub-command registration
patterns.description: Cmd is a package-level *cobra.Command; sub-commands are registered in init() via Cmd.AddCommand. This differs from the entitlement package's RootCommand() pattern — match the style of the file you are extending.

patterns.name: Delegate to internal.App service, never construct locally
patterns.description: RunE must call internal.App.LLMCostSyncJob.Run(cmd.Context()) directly; never construct llmcost.SyncJob or its dependencies inside the command.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'llm-cost' parent command and its 'sync' sub-command; canonical example of the init()-based sub-command registration pattern.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LLMCostSyncJob must exist on internal.App (wired via common.LLMCost in wire.go); removing it from Application breaks this command at compile time.

anti_patterns: Constructing llmcost.SyncJob directly instead of using internal.App.LLMCostSyncJob
Adding business logic beyond delegating to the sync job

code_examples.scenario: Adding a sub-command that delegates to a Wire-provided job

cmd/jobs/migrate.purpose: Cobra sub-command for running database migrations via the Wire-provided Migrator; validates the migration mode flag before executing and explicitly rejects the 'job' mode to prevent circular migration loops.

patterns.name: Override App.Migrator.Config at runtime before Migrate
patterns.description: The --mode flag value is parsed via config.AutoMigrate() and written to internal.App.Migrator.Config.AutoMigrate before calling Migrate. This is the only place in jobs that mutates App state after initialization.

patterns.name: Validate mode before execution, rejecting 'job' mode
patterns.description: config.AutoMigrate() parses and validates the mode string; the command errors if the mode is disabled and explicitly rejects AutoMigrateMigrationJob to prevent recursive use of the jobs binary for job-mode migration.
patterns.example:
if !migrationMode.Enabled() { return fmt.Errorf("migration mode is disabled") }
if migrationMode == config.AutoMigrateMigrationJob { return fmt.Errorf("migration mode cannot be job for this command") }

key_file_guides.role: Implements the 'migrate' Cobra command (RootCommand()), wrapping common.Migrator with mode validation. Default mode is 'migration' (config.AutoMigrateMigration).
key_file_guides.watch_for: The --mode flag accepts only 'ent' and 'migration'; 'job' is explicitly rejected. Do not add DDL or data transforms here — those belong in tools/migrate/migrations/.

anti_patterns: Calling tools/migrate directly instead of going through internal.App.Migrator
Adding migration logic (DDL, data transforms) to this command — those belong in tools/migrate/migrations/
Allowing the 'job' migration mode through without rejection

decisions.decision: Migration mode is overridden on App.Migrator.Config rather than constructing a new Migrator.
decisions.rationale: The Wire-provided Migrator already has the correct DB client and logger; overriding just the mode field reuses all that wiring without duplication.

code_examples.scenario: The migrate command's mode validation and execution
